senses.definition: economical and not expensive
an establishment that provides vacationers with lodging, food, entertainment, etc.

examples.x: They stayed at a luxurious beach resort during their vacation.
The resort offers spa treatments to help guests unwind and relax.
We are planning to visit a mountain resort for our family reunion.
The resort has multiple restaurants, pools, and golf courses for guests to enjoy.

senses.definition: a place that provides travelers with temporary accommodation

examples.x: After a long day of travel, they were relieved to reach their lodging.
We booked our lodging near the beach to enjoy easy access to the sea.
The city offers various lodging options, from budget hostels to luxury hotels.

senses.definition: a comfortable area, often in an airport or hotel, where people can relax, wait, or socialize, typically offering seating, refreshments, and sometimes Wi-Fi

examples.x: We waited in the airport lounge until our flight was called.
The hotel lounge had a cozy atmosphere with plush couches and soft lighting.
Business travelers often use the airport lounge to catch up on work before flights.
They met in the lounge of the hotel for drinks before dinner.
The airline offers access to its exclusive lounge for first-class passengers.

senses.definition: the time when a guest should leave a hotel room, pay the bills, and return the key

examples.x: If you need assistance with your luggage during checkout, our staff will be happy to assist you.
Don't forget to return your room key during checkout to avoid any key replacement fees.
During checkout, guests have the opportunity to provide feedback on their stay through our satisfaction survey.

senses.definition: someone who is staying in a hotel room

examples.x: The hotel resident enjoyed complimentary breakfast during her stay.
As a resident, he received special offers for extended stays at the hotel.
The friendly staff made the resident feel welcome and comfortable.
Each resident was given a key card to access their room.
The hotel organized events for residents to socialize and relax.

senses.definition: a hotel service where guests can order food, drinks, or other amenities to be delivered to their room, typically from a menu provided by the hotel

examples.x: We ordered breakfast through room service because we wanted to relax in our room.
The hotel offers 24-hour room service for guests who prefer to dine in their rooms.
Room service is convenient for travelers arriving late at night who are hungry.
She called room service to request extra towels and toiletries.
The room service menu included a variety of options, from snacks to full meals.

senses.definition: a business that makes arrangements for people who want to travel

examples.x: They used a travel agency to book their vacation package to Hawaii.
The travel agency provided recommendations for hotels and activities based on their preferences.
She works at a travel agency that specializes in arranging luxury cruises.
The travel agency offers personalized services to ensure clients have a stress-free travel experience.
Online travel agencies have made it easier to compare prices and book trips from anywhere.

senses.definition: an online ticket that can be received electronically instead of a paper ticket

examples.x: She received her E-ticket via email for the concert she was attending.
The airline allows passengers to check in using their e-ticket on their mobile devices.
With an E-ticket, there is no need to print a physical copy for entry.
The e-ticket included a QR code for easy admission to the venue.
He scanned his E-ticket at the airport for quick boarding.

senses.definition: a person who is on vacation or holiday, typically traveling away from home for leisure or relaxation

examples.x: The beach was crowded with vacationers enjoying the sunny weather.
As vacationers, they spent their days exploring the city's historic sites.
Vacationers often seek out new experiences and cultural attractions while traveling.
He met fellow vacationers at the hotel's poolside bar and exchanged travel stories.
The resort offered various activities to keep vacationers entertained throughout their stay.

senses.definition: a short trip taken for pleasure, particularly one arranged for a group of people

examples.x: The school organized an excursion to the science museum for the students.
They planned a weekend excursion to the nearby national park.
The cruise included several excursions to explore different islands.
The company arranged an excursion for employees to promote team building.
The family took an excursion to the beach, enjoying the sun and sand.

senses.definition: a vacation arranged by a travel agent or a company at a fixed price including the cost of transport, accommodations, etc.

examples.x: She booked a package tour to explore several countries in Europe.
The travel agency advertised a package tour that included guided sightseeing.
A package tour can often provide savings compared to booking each component separately.
They enjoyed a package tour that featured cultural experiences and local cuisine.
Families often prefer package tours for convenience and planning ease.

senses.definition: to go on vacation away from home

examples.x: Every winter, they dream of getting away to a warm, tropical paradise.

senses.definition: to go for a long walk or journey, particularly in the mountains, forests, etc. as an adventure

examples.x: The group of hikers decided to trek through the rugged mountains to reach the remote village.
The explorers trekked across the frozen tundra, facing bitter cold and strong winds.
They trekked up the steep trail in the early morning darkness.
The wildlife researchers trekked deep into the jungle to study the behavior of rare species.
Inspired by adventure stories, the friends planned to trek through the dense forest.

senses.definition: a narrow passage in a theater, train, aircraft, etc. that separates rows of seats

examples.x: The flight attendant walked down the aisle of the airplane, serving refreshments to the passengers seated on either side.
In the theater, he found his seat by walking down the aisle and maneuvering past several rows of occupied seats.
The train conductor moved up and down the aisle, checking tickets and assisting passengers with their luggage.
They sat in the middle seats, so they had to get up and step into the aisle each time someone needed to pass by.
Please keep the aisle clear for safety reasons.

senses.definition: the area where passengers sit in an airplane

examples.x: The cabin of the airplane was spacious, with overhead bins for luggage.
The first-class cabin featured wider seats and additional legroom.
The cabin was equipped with entertainment systems for passenger enjoyment.
He walked down the aisle in the cabin to use the restroom.
He found his seat in the front of the cabin.

senses.definition: the group of people whose job is looking after the passengers on an aircraft

examples.x: The cabin crew welcomed passengers aboard with friendly smiles and safety instructions.
Throughout the flight, the cabin crew served meals and beverages to the travelers.
The cabin crew conducted safety demonstrations before takeoff.
She admired how the cabin crew handled an emergency situation with professionalism.
He admired the efficiency of the cabin crew during the long flight.

senses.definition: the area at an airport where passengers can collect their cases, bags, etc. after they land

examples.x: After landing, travelers headed to the baggage claim to collect their bags.
The baggage claim area was crowded with passengers waiting for their luggage.
She spotted her suitcase on the conveyor belt at the baggage claim.
The airport provided clear signs to guide passengers to the baggage claim.
Delayed flights often lead to longer waits at the baggage claim.

senses.definition: the confusion and tiredness one can experience after a long flight, particularly when rapidly traveling across multiple time zones

examples.x: After flying from New York to Tokyo, she experienced severe jet lag for several days.
He struggled with jet lag after returning from his business trip to Europe.
Jet lag can affect athletes traveling to compete in international tournaments.
Tips for minimizing jet lag include staying hydrated and gradually adjusting sleep schedules before travel.
The effects of jet lag can vary depending on the direction of travel and individual resilience.

senses.definition: a building where trains, buses, planes, or ships start or finish their journey

examples.x: There were shops and restaurants inside the terminal for passengers to enjoy.
He met his friend at the terminal after a long journey.
A taxi stand is located just outside the terminal.

senses.definition: an important railroad line between two cities or large towns

examples.x: The train's main line connects the city to several suburban areas.
The main line upgrades included new tracks and improved safety measures.
The main line runs between the capital city and the industrial center, facilitating commerce.
Many businesses rely on the main line for transporting goods efficiently between regions.
Travelers often prefer the main line for its frequent train services and convenience.

senses.definition: to go on vacation by a ship or boat

examples.x: For their anniversary, the couple decided to cruise around the Caribbean.
Instead of the usual road trip, the family opted to cruise along the European coastline.
The retirees planned to cruise across the Mediterranean, visiting historical sites.
They cruised on a luxury liner with many activities.
The family decided to cruise instead of flying.

senses.definition: to arrive later than expected or planned

examples.x: The flight delayed due to heavy fog.
He often delays when it comes to making decisions.
She delays when it's time to leave for school.
The train usually delays during rush hour.

senses.definition: to travel across or on an area of water by a ship or boat

examples.x: The cruise ship successfully navigated through the narrow channels of the fjord.
Yachtsmen often compete in regattas, showcasing their ability to navigate through a designated course in open waters.
In the era of tall ships, sailors used stars to determine their position at sea, allowing them to navigate across vast distances.
The maritime pilot skillfully navigated into the harbor.

senses.definition: a vehicle that is pulled by a car, in which people can sleep and live, used particularly for traveling and camping

examples.x: They traveled across the country in a caravan equipped with beds and a kitchenette.
The caravan was parked in a scenic spot near the lake for the weekend.
Our family enjoys camping in a caravan because it provides comfort and mobility.
The caravan included solar panels to provide electricity while off-grid.
They rented a spacious caravan for their road trip through Europe.

senses.definition: a place at which a road meets a railroad, typically marked by gates, signals, or warning signs to alert drivers and pedestrians of approaching trains

examples.x: He stopped his car at the railroad crossing when the barriers started to lower.
The railroad crossing was equipped with flashing lights and bells to warn drivers of oncoming trains.
Pedestrians must wait behind the designated line at the railroad crossing until the train passes.
The town installed new safety features at the railroad crossing to prevent accidents.
Drivers are required to check both ways at the railroad crossing before proceeding across the tracks.

senses.definition: any of the separate sections within a passenger train carriage, typically enclosed by walls and equipped with seats

examples.x: We booked a private compartment for our overnight train journey.
The first-class compartment was spacious and comfortable with reclining seats.
Passengers enjoyed privacy in their compartments during the long-distance trip.
Each compartment had its own window for passengers to enjoy the scenery.

senses.definition: an area near a road where people can stop to eat food, rest, etc.

examples.x: The family decided to stop at a rest stop to stretch their legs during the long drive.
Many rest stops offer vending machines for quick snacks and drinks.
The clean rest stop had ample parking and picnic tables for visitors.
Some rest stops include tourist information centers to help travelers plan their route.
The rest stop featured a playground, making it a great place for kids to play.

senses.definition: a store where small items and souvenirs are sold, suitable for giving as presents

examples.x: The gift shop at the museum featured unique items related to the exhibits.
She found the perfect birthday present at the local gift shop.
The gift shop offered personalized items for special occasions.
Tourists often visit the gift shop to buy souvenirs from their trip.
The charming gift shop had a selection of handmade crafts and candles.

senses.definition: an extremely large balloon filled with heated air, which enables it to float and travel through the sky

examples.x: They enjoyed a scenic ride in a hot-air balloon over the countryside.
The hot-air balloon gently ascended into the sky as the burner heated the air inside.
Hot-air balloons are popular for sightseeing and aerial photography.
The crew carefully deflated the hot-air balloon after the flight.
She fulfilled her dream of flying in a hot-air balloon during her vacation.

senses.definition: the 1st of April on which people in Western countries play tricks on one another and the person who is tricked is called the April Fool
the 4th of July on which Americans celebrate the declaration of independence from Britain in 1776

examples.x: Families often gather for picnics and outdoor activities on Independence Day.
The city hosts a large parade featuring floats and marching bands for Independence Day.
Many people display flags and decorations to honor Independence Day.
The historical significance of Independence Day is highlighted in educational events and ceremonies.

senses.definition: the evening of 31st of December, which is the last day of the year
a cultural and religious holiday celebrated on the 17th of March, honoring Saint Patrick who helped establish Christianity in Ireland
the day following Thanksgiving in the US, traditionally marked by massive discounts and sales events across stores, marking the beginning of the Christmas shopping season

examples.x: Shoppers flock to stores early in the morning on Black Friday to take advantage of the deep discounts.
Many retailers offer online Black Friday deals in addition to in-store promotions.
Black Friday is known for its crowded malls and long lines at checkout counters.
Consumers plan their purchases in advance to make the most of Black Friday savings.
Some stores extend their Black Friday sales throughout the weekend, known as Cyber Weekend.

senses.definition: the day before the start of Lent, on which people in some countries begin celebrating by dancing in the streets with loud music and wearing colorful costumes

examples.x: Mardi Gras is celebrated with various cultural traditions, reflecting the area's history.
Tourists travel from all over to experience the excitement of Mardi Gras in New Orleans.
Participants in Mardi Gras often throw beads and trinkets to parade-goers from floats.
The city streets come alive with festive decorations and themed events for Mardi Gras.

senses.definition: the act or business of carrying people or goods from one place to another by different means

examples.x: Transportation of goods by truck is common in the logistics industry.
The transportation sector plays a vital role in the economy.
The transportation industry has adapted to include rideshare services.
Logistics companies specialize in the transportation of products across regions.
The transportation of fresh produce often requires refrigerated trucks.
